Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Continued To May-14-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gerald Woods for Michael Poston (Petitioner/Administrator).
Marilyn Young former Counsel for the Co-Administrators.
William Fishman for Alicia Whitehall and Heather McIsaac.
Robert Poston also present.

Robert stated that he is not sure if he is here to contest the petition for suspension and that he has no objection to Ms. Young being relieved as his Counsel. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel as to Robert Poston (order for the same signed in open Court). Robert Poston waives notice of the order. The Court noted that the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel as to Michael Poston is moot and off calendar as a Substitution of Attorney has been filed. Mr. Woods stated that they still do not have all of the documents signed by Robert, that they have a third inventory and appraisal, and an accounting that need to be signed, and that if signed he can file a petition for final distribution within a few weeks. Robert stated that he intends to contest the petition and that he is prepared to sign the documents if has information available to verify the documents. The Court stated that in light of Robert Poston's intent to contest the petition, it is continuing the matter for filing of written objections. Mr. Woods suggested that the Parties go down to the records department to review the other inventory and appraisals that Robert has signed. The Petition for Suspension of Powers and Removal of Personal Representative and status on close of estate are continued to May 14, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encourages the Parties to go down and review documents.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel--Civil
Off Calendar Apr-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Apr-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marilyn Young for and with Michael Poston.
David Turpin appeared by telephone for Trust & Estate Search LLC.

The Court stated that it is not in a position to suspend Robert as Co-Administrator, questions service of the citation and questions whether it is ethically appropriate for Ms. Young to continue representing Michael without an informed waiver from Robert. Ms. Young stated that the citation was served on February 1st on Robert's Son, that Robert has not returned the citation, that she wrote to both of her Clients in October, that Robert is non-responsive, that they have an inventory and appraisal, but without all of the signatures she can not file it and that they are so close to closing out the estate. Mr. Turpin stated that his Client wants to see this closed, that there has been no progress for one year, that he has the same concerns as the Court does with regard to Ms. Young continuing to represent Robert and raised the issue of the Court on its own motion suspending Robert and appointing the Public Administrator. The Court stated that it will not do that and that perhaps Mr. Turpin's Client can file something. Discussion was held with respect to Ms. Young not representing both of her Clients, Michael getting independent representation, having to re-serve the citation, the statutory fees being split between Ms. Young and new Counsel, etc. The Petition for Suspension of Powers, etc., status on close of estate and the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel are continued to April 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Continued To Oct-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for (co-Administrators) Robert Poston and Michael Poston (who is present).

Ms. Young reported that she sent another copy of the documents to be signed by Robert (via mail and email) and he has not returned them, that a cousin (David) has indicated that Robert told him he would sign the documents, that it has been 1 week with nothing returned, that they want him removed as administrator, that there is a bond payment due in two days for a bond that is much more than the remaining balance and they would like to reduce the bond but cannot without Robert's signature. Ms. Young further reported that they will be filing an ex parte petition to reduce bond as well as a petition for Robert to be removed and asked if the court would allow Michael only to sign the petition to reduce bond. The Court replied that it cannot and that there is nothing before it to suspend or remove Robert. Michael Poston stated that he wants Robert removed immediately. The Court stated that this request is not before it today. The Status on Close of Estate was continued to October 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Sep-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marilyn Young for Michael Poston.
William Fishman appeared by telephone for Heather McIsaac and Alicia Whitehall.
David Turpin appeared by telephone for Trust & Estate Research.

The Court stated that it needs an inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution. Ms. Young stated that she has the third appraisal, that she has not filed it because only one of the Administrators has signed it, that the assets have been appraised by Mr. Wong and valued at $27,713.00 and that Mr. Wong has been paid for his services. Ms. Young also stated that she has a petition for final distribution drafted, that the accounting is close to completion, and that she has a few holes and needs information from the Co-Administrators. In addition, Ms. Young stated that there are some communication issues between the Administrators, that she copies of e-mails, that she called the office of the Administrator that did not sign the inventory yesterday and that she did not get a response. Mr. Turpin stated that he can only foresee the same problem when it comes time to sign the accounting. The Court raised the possibility of needing to file some other form of petition in the event the Co-Administrator does not intend to cooperate. Status on close of estate is continued to September 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects an inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration to be filed by then.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Apr-10-2018 Continued To May-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for Co-Administrators
William Fishman for Heather McIsacc and Alicia Whitehall (Beneficiaries)

Ms. Young stated that her petition to approve status report is being circulated, that she has obtained one signature and waiting for Bob's signature. Ms. Young also stated that she is waiting for a 3rd and final inventory and appraisal, there are 700 shares of stocks in book entry form, Probate referee has requested additional information regarding the production of the 2 Wills in Los Angeles County, and can file first and final accounting when the inventory and appraisal gets back. Ms. Young further stated that 25% interest to one of the beneficiary was assigned to an heir finder, that the Court found the heir finder fee excessive and reduced the 29% to 18%, and requested that the remaining 11% go to Ms. Montoya's 2 daughters. Mr. Fishman inquired about the distribution of the 11% held back. The Court stated that none of this is before the Court. The Status on Close of Estate is continued to May 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration or a Petition for Final Distribution.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution, And For Allowance On Account Of Compensation To Attorney
Off Calendar Jan-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Turpin for Trust and Estate Search.
Marilyn Young for Co-Petitioners.
William Fishman for Heather McIsaac and Alicia Whitehall (Assignees).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the amount of fees/percentage sought by Trust and Estate Search is grossly unreasonable. Mr. Turpin explained services performed, spoke of the Investigator's process and assistance he provided to Ms. Montoya, cited other cases where heir hunters with higher percentages were upheld, etc. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc. is taken under submission. Mr. Turpin requested a statement of decision.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Reporter: Nancy Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 510-757-8550.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution, And For Allowance On Account Of Compensation To Attorney
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Preliminary Distribution, And For Allowance On Account Of Compensation To Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Continued To Dec-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marilyn Young for Co-Petitioners.
William Fishman for Alicia Whitehall and Heather McIsaac (Assignees).
David Tuprin for Trust & Estate Search.

The Court raised the issue of the 29% fee for Trust and Estate Search, and stated that it has an obligation to inquire as to whether or not this is grossly unreasonable. Mr. Turpin raised the issue of the Client being noticed on this inquiry, stated that they have begun some discussion, that there are two components, one being the missing heir and the other being how difficult it was to find the heir. The Court stated that a declaration would be helpful, and that it does not know how many hours were spent and what was done to find the heir. Mr. Turpin stated that he would be happy to submit a declaration and that he would like three or four weeks to do so. Ms. Young stated that she sent a copy of the hand written notes to Counsel yesterday, that the Family is in agreement that it is the Decedent's writing, but it is not signed and that they will proceed in intestacy. All parties indicated that they are in agreement. With respect to final distribution, Ms. Young stated that there are 700 shares of stocks, that information was not available, that they are oil wells and mineral rights, that they will be in the third partial inventory, and that the Family hopes the Court will distribute 71% (preliminary distribution) of the Estate and hold 29%. The Court stated that it will grant preliminary distribution on everything except for 29%. Ms. Young is to prepare a revised form of order. The bifurcated issue of the 29% to the heir finder is continued to December 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
